title: 10 medicinal constituents of Ervatamia coronaria  (queen of the night)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Ervatamia coronaria alkaloids is a group of bioactive compounds isolated from its roots and stems. These alkaloids have been found to exhibit diverse biological activities, including antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Specifically, they have shown potential in treating diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disorders, and neurological disorders.

Ervatamia coronaria, also known as Queen of the Night, quinolones is a type of plant-based antimicrobial agent. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various infections and diseases. The quinolones isolated from this plant have been found to exhibit significant antibacterial activity against a range of microorganisms, including multidrug-resistant bacteria. This has made Ervatamia coronaria an attractive source for the development of new antibiotics and antimicrobial agents.

Ervatamia coronaria phenolic acids is a type of bioactive compound found in the leaves and stems of this plant species. These phenolic acids have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, making them potentially useful in the prevention and treatment of various diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disorders.

Ervatamia coronaria, also known as Queen of the Night, flavonoids is a group of plant compounds found in its leaves and flowers. These flavonoids have been isolated and identified, displaying significant antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The most prominent flavonoids present are eriodyctiol, quercetin, and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess potent free radical scavenging properties and exhibit cytotoxic effects against various cancer cell lines.

Ervatamia coronaria terpenoids is a complex mixture of compounds found in its leaves and stems. These terpenoids have been shown to possess a range of biological activities, including antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The major component of Ervatamia coronaria terpenoids is a sesquiterpene named euryopsenin, which has been found to exhibit potent cytotoxicity against human cancer cells.

Ervatamia coronaria saponins is a group of compounds extracted from the roots and stems of this plant species. These saponins have been found to exhibit various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, they have been shown to have potential therapeutic applications in treating diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disorders.

Ervatamia coronaria, also known as Queen of the Night, aldehydes is a class of organic compounds found in its flowers. These aldehydes have been reported to possess various bioactive properties, including antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The most prominent aldehyde compound isolated from E. coronaria is coronarinal, which has been shown to exhibit significant cytotoxicity against certain cancer cell lines.

Ervatamia coronaria esters is a type of natural compound extracted from the plant's flowers. These esters are responsible for the sweet, floral scent emitted by the plant, particularly at night when the flowers bloom. The esters have been shown to possess potential pharmacological activities, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Ervatamia coronaria ketones is a type of natural compound found in the leaves and stems of this flowering plant. These ketones have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The unique chemical structure of Ervatamia coronaria ketones makes them attractive for further research and possible applications in medicine and cosmetics.

Ervatamia coronaria hydroxybenzoates is a type of chemical compound found in its leaves and stems. These hydroxybenzoates have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making them valuable in various applications such as food preservation, pharmaceuticals, and even cosmetics.

intro: Ervatamia coronaria has active constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making this plant a potential remedy for various ailments. The alkaloids, in particular, have been shown to exhibit sedative and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate anxiety and chronic pain. For instance, the flavonoids may help improve cardiovascular health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, while the terpenoids could potentially treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma.
